Good morning,
After reviewing scrap from all three shifts, we found that the 3rd shift has 1,516 lbs of physical scrap, while only 990 lbs (chocolate, cream, and caramel) were recorded in SafetyChain.
We will adjust the system based on the physical inventory. Please ensure the actual scrap amount is entered into SafetyChain to avoid large inventory adjustments, especially for chocolate.

Thank you. Alan Greer Kimberly Morales Rose Smuin

Alan Greer 
Please speak with your team. This is the 2nd time scrap has been reported incorrectly.
The final piece has no stains
Line has been running very well all morning with no issues to report on both WDS and TG area.
Viscosity
Final pallet check

After completing the full inventory of Item 10169 (Chocolate used in ML4), four pallets were found to be missing (R247209, R288296, R292123, R293495). The investigation determined that they were consumed but were not recorded in the system. One pallet was confirmed by a used label found in the waste, and the other three are reflected in the production report but were never entered into the system. An inventory adjustment will be made to align the system with the physical inventory. Rose Smuin Itzel Chavez Marcos Montano Alan Greer Nidia Lopez Corea
Catch tray between south swecos clean ✅

Left over barrels of Affogato cream and caramel have been re-weighted, and re labeled with the actual quantities. Operators will place them back in the warmroom.

Test passed
Level 2 clean completed and passed off ATP.
Conner Boyd Caleb York is this the first Cleaning Level 4 with this MD?
Didn't we do one last week or so?
I think last week was Level 3.
Pretzels to S’mores?

We completed 3, level 4 cleans, when it was a brand new belt before introducing it to the line. 

However, this is the first level 4 we’ve done after running product on it.

I believe it was 3rd shift that completed the level 3 for MD on that clean. I plan to ask Luis what parts were necessary to remove during his level 3 for it. 
Charity Young

Since this is first level 4 clean of MD equipment - I realized we might not fully know how much needs disassembled to get clean. 

Does maintenance have a process for what is needed? 

Caleb York Chris Jeffs
Thank you for calling this out, I’ll be sure to follow-up with our team further.

There should be a lock on the MD when we are removing covers.





And same for the C1 belt as well.

North End roller: 
This roller may be removable, but it has not been done before. 

I spoke with Quality, and we’re going to try and clean this roller without removal.

They are a few more areas where the standard for removal or how to remove is still murky: 



(South and North side nose bars)



(White roller in the center of the MD)

These areas are made even more difficult to access due to the tension. I’m checking with maintenance to see if that’s something we can adjust further on it. 
Dakota Adair Charity Young Chris Jeffs Caleb York
Was level 3
I would rather have a decision from a Chris before taking random stuff off, but taking off tension is doable

Maintenance has reached out to the Chris Jeffs Chris Givens to get approval on which parts can be removed. 

We will continue cleaning the areas we can reach until we hear back on the areas that can be disassembled. 
Dakota Adair Curt Willis

Thank you! 
Chris Givens Caleb York
I will do what I can, but the tension roller doesn't come out that friendly
Caleb York will remove tension roller on the MD.  This will give you more slack on the belt and you will be able to clean all the other rollers in place.😊
Thank you Chris Givens
Thank you Caleb York
Even with the tension roller removed, it doesn't give much more access to the inner rollers/belt

Here’s the limited window of access we have to reach inside to clean 2 white rollers, and the drive(blue) roller at the center of the MD. 



(Center of MD)



(Roller and scraper) under South side nose bar. 

To meet the standard of a level 4 will be difficult for these areas under these conditions. 

The team has been coached to continue to clean the areas we can access, while also being reminded to rotate with each other when cleaning the tight spaces. 
Dakota Adair Charity Young, Curt Willis, Victor Mejia
This needs to be visual clean.

Those are the areas hard to reach and unable to do a proper visual inspection 


Charity Young Conner Boyd
Yes, I am following the process and Dakota Adair has been reaching out to me

Caleb York Charity Young I have spoken to Chris Givens. Caleb is aware on how to take the MD apart so we can properly clean.  Give him a some time as I know he is working on the Matrix machines. If we are not able to get it apart, please contact Chris Givens so he can support. If we need to get it cleaned first thing Monday morning. Please reach out. Curt Willis please be aware
Thank you Caleb
I will try to take out one more roller once I return from lunch

Chris Givens and I have talked and he has given the team instructions that will hopefully get more room for cleaning.

Conner Boyd know to keep cleaning and that it needs to pass the level 4 clean 

If needed this will roll into 3rd shift and Chris will plan on being in early on Monday if we are not able to meet cleaning standards by the end of 3rd shift

While scrubbing the buffer, one of our employees a blue scraper at the bottom of AD4. 
(Nut Allergen Dark)  

Maintenance is checking to see if there’s a tool we can use to fish it out. 
Curt Willis Dakota Adair
I have removed 3 rollers in total now, it should have enough slack now. One thing of note, do not soak the rollers in water. Each roller has 6 bearings in it, the water will ruin them.
I've been looking for the long grabber arm tool, but I've no clue where it's gone. It was in the shop the other day....
Me, Bryan and the new guy fished it out
The C1 belt will not run until this plug is replaced.

Maintenance is in the process of replacing this plug. 

ATP and Allergen have passed for the belt,  all that remains is the “run belt for 5 minutes” step which will be completed once the plug is replaced. 
Luis Falcon Curt Willis
Plug has been fixed. And we will now conduct the run for 5 minute test.
Nut allergen filters
Jar Label setup is done
We cleaned hard-to-reach areas on MD, though there are still spots we couldn't get to; QA said they would come by on Monday to check, so we weren't able to do much assembly. Curt Willis Jody Howse
Everything is off or night mode
The pouch meets the specifications. The lote code and best-by date are correct.
The weight of the pouches is within the range, and the number of pieces meets the target (8).
The pallet meets the specifications. The box label and the final label match.

Trimmed about 1" off the upper funnel so the lower one can be raised and not jam up the pouch opening. They are not making metal to metal contact either now that it's trimmed. Should solve the funnel jams now and I'll keep an eye on it.
The pouch weight remained within the range, and the piece count met the target (8). It is running normally and in accordance with specifications.
Level 2 clean completed and passed off ATP
MFM changeover parts are now organized and in the cabinet
Piece count: 30
The Tunnel 3 belt is starting to stop constantly.
We will inspect the rollers and the motor
Tunnel #3 the belt is slipping, it's happened to us twice already, we're going to stop the line to clean the rollers. Justin Garner Curt Willis
We already clean the rollers and motor the line is running right now.

We're having problems with the Matrix 6 and 8; the bags won't open all the way.  The poches are partially zipped out of the box causing issues with pouch opening. The product dumps onto unopened pouches. Maintenance has spent a signifcant amount of time making adjustments to cam settings and pouch opening arms. Maintenance beloves the pouches are the issue. The same symptoms on all three matrizx machines. Operator verofied cemterlines. The pouches  are getting sealed when it passes through the Sealer.
Do we have a different lot of bags to see if they are defective? Victor Romero Sr.
We're trying to use it with another pallet of pouches.
The other pallet of bags 41021 we have on hand is from the same lot.
Curt Willis Victor Romero Sr.what’s the plan and how can we help? What scrap rate are we getting with this issue? Janell Scharrer
End of 1shift

For an entire cycle the MM will be hitting on every pouch, but then 1-3 will be marked as detected when the pouch isn’t open. 

Caleb York is on his way to look at it. Then, we’ll grab our centerlines from yesterday and take a look at those to see if anything is different and post back shortly. 
Curt Willis Aubrey Howe

After comparing our Center Lines from yesterday one of the differences we noticed was the “Zip-Opening” setting being set to “Inside” when yesterday we had it set to “Outside”. 

We’ll see if that adjustment has any effect. 
Curt Willis Dakota Adair

Aubrey Howe

I have the team opening the pouches, but they are saying that they are resealing as they go into the machine.

Conner Boyd as has a action to take pictures and videos of what they are seeing and the maintenance team is making it their focus.

We will reevaluate after an hour to see next steps

Conner Boyd

How are we looking on bags now?
The only ones that fail to open are zipped already. With the zip setting on inner, outer or both, it cannot open the bags if they're zipped already.

We now have an employee who’s opening the pouches to prevent zipped bags from going through, and I’ll get a count shortly. 
Curt Willis
Enrober in reheat
Mini temper in reheat
Temper unit in reheat
Why is everything in reheat?
The team stopped cooking caramel, and went into a re-heat.

They can have caramel ready and sent to the line in 1HR-1HR 30min. 

Do we still want to run for the 2- 2 1/2 HRs?
Curt Willis
Nope

Team, we didn't receive the pecan pallets from L3 for Monday's production on Sol 3. Jazmin ordered the pallets to be delivered today at 11 pm, but we never received them. Curt Willis
Rose Smuin Laura Lake can we look into what happened here please and what we can do to have them here Monday morning

Ok, I see pallets now.  I saw a request for 11 pm from Jazmin from L3 of the 41054. So I was thinking maybe that was it. Sorry for the confusion.

Area organized, please keep it that way! Thank you

Rose Smuin just to make sure everyone is on the same page in sharepoint are we using "supervisor toolkit" or "supervisor toolkit 2". It looks like information has been being filled out on the shift exchange in supervisor toolkit 2 however to access RCA documents you have to use just the regular supervisor toolkit folder.
Everyone needs to use sharepoint

yes both of those folder are in sharepoint, the question is because RCA is only available in supervisor toolkit (original) while the shift exchange is being updated in supervisor toolkit 2. To eliminate confusion we need to combine them into one where we can access both documents without needing to jump around. Before everything was just in supervisor toolkit (original)

This is a good callout, nathan. As far as I know, there's a combination of documents that have been copied into either folder. Tomorrow morning I was planning to clean up the shift exchange form and copy it over into the original supervisor toolkit folder
We are using the files in "Supervisor Toolkit (1)" since it isn't missing anything. I copied over Friday and Saturday sheets so everything is in there except for Thursday.
Nice. Thanks
Tunnel 1 on S2 AREA

KEEPING curtains closed & taped is not specific to MDLZ or any other customer — it is specific to the conditions while manufacturing. Please have your teams keep an eye on these areas so that they are taped immediately and not days later.  Thank you 🙏
